facts: Booked two studios (17k points/night each) at Suncadia Resort. Hotel called before arrival offering to merge into 2BR suite for $100/night extra; OP refused. Upon arrival, hotel claimed no rooms available and offered a house in an annex building (no pool access, 10-min walk in 5°C weather). OP had called twice beforehand to confirm not being moved, but hotel denied knowledge. Negotiation lasted 15 minutes with OP recording video, insisting on original booking. Eventually hotel found two upgraded rooms in main building.
actual_outcome: Full 68k points refunded (17k x 2 rooms x 2 nights), $500 dining credit, both rooms upgraded in main lodge.
evidence_used: video recording of front desk conversation
reusable_lesson: Do not check in until terms are settled. Video recording the conversation is extremely effective. Hotels always have reserve rooms - insist on original booking. Be clear about your bottom line.
notes: Detailed negotiation play-by-play with outcome. Hotel was overbooked due to a local event.

brand_or_airline: Hyatt Place Kyoto → Hyatt Regency Kyoto
facts: Arrived at Hyatt Place Kyoto after mobile check-in, only to be told hotel was oversold. Walked to Hyatt Regency Kyoto with hotel covering taxi fare. OP had already mailed luggage to HP and had to buy replacement toiletries.
actual_outcome: Taxi fare covered by hotel. Free drinks, 2pm late checkout, free breakfast at Regency. No additional points. Most forum members felt compensation was reasonable.
escalation_path: Hyatt concierge line (considered but not used)
reusable_lesson: Mobile check-in does not guarantee a room. If walk involves moving to a different area, insist on luggage transfer assistance.
notes: Limited compensation because walk was to a higher-category hotel. Japanese hotel practices differ from North America.

facts: Arrived at 11:30pm to a pre-booked Crowne Plaza. Front desk said 'we are overbooked' and security guard suggested checking a nearby motel. OP was Platinum Elite member. IHG has a Reservation Guarantee that should have provided alternate comparable accommodation. OP used ChatGPT to draft a complaint to IHG customer service.
actual_outcome: Received 15k IHG points (~$75 value) after complaining. Forum members felt this was insufficient given Platinum status.
reusable_lesson: IHG's Online Reservation Guarantee requires the hotel to provide alternate comparable accommodation + transport + first night free. Always escalate to corporate if front desk is dismissive.
notes: OP was entitled to more under IHG policy but settled for 15k points.

facts: Booked Holiday Inn Springfield for 2 nights (17k + 22k points). Arrived 10:30pm, told hotel was full. Handwritten note sent to adjacent Crown Plaza. Crown Plaza hadn't been notified. No bottled water provided despite 3 requests. OP later discovered IHG had refunded all points.
actual_outcome: Full points refunded (39k points) - both nights comped. No additional compensation.
evidence_used: handwritten note from Holiday Inn front desk
reusable_lesson: IHG policy covers first night at comparable hotel + transport. Points reservations when walked may result in automatic refund - check your account. Always verify the receiving hotel has been notified.
notes: Full points refund without even asking. Walk was handled poorly (handwritten note, no notification to receiving hotel).

brand_or_airline: Hotel Figueroa (Unbound Collection) → The Hoxton, Downtown LA
facts: Booked Hotel Figueroa for 20k points (cash rate $700+) at Hyatt. Hotel called ahead to say sold out, offered to move to The Hoxton, Downtown LA ($606/night cash rate) and refund points. OP was concerned about losing elite benefits (free parking, breakfast, late checkout). Negotiated before accepting.
actual_outcome: 20k points refunded (full), 5k additional goodwill points, parking and breakfast differential refunded. Effectively free night at comparable hotel + 5k bonus points.
reusable_lesson: Being contacted ahead of time gives leverage - negotiate before accepting. Always quantify lost elite benefits (parking, breakfast) and ask for compensation. 5k points is a reasonable ask for inconvenience.
notes: OP was proactive and negotiated before arrival. Outcome was positive.

facts: Booked LH first class IST-FRA-SFO via Aeroplan. LH notified of delay at 3am in Istanbul, causing missed connection in FRA. LH offered UA business same day or LH first next day. OP chose next day. Arrived SFO ~24 hours late. Filed EU261 claim for €600.
actual_outcome: LH initially denied claiming 'flight irregularities'. OP complained to German LBA. After 6 months, LH paid €600 (~$700).
reusable_lesson: EU261 applies to EU airlines even for non-EU to non-EU via EU transit. If airline denies, complain to the national aviation authority of the EU country where the airline is based. German LBA processes complaints efficiently.
notes: Excellent step-by-step DP with timeline. 6-month process but paid after regulatory pressure.

facts: Family of 3 booked award premium economy CDG-EWR on AF. Canceled due to Paris snowstorm. AF rebooked to CDG-JFK 2 days later. Second flight also canceled. Total delay ~48 hours. Filed EU261 claims.
actual_outcome: AF paid €600 per person (€1,800 total) plus ~€1,800 for hotel, meals, transport. Total ~$3,600. Also filed Amex Platinum trip delay claim.
reusable_lesson: EU261 applies even for weather if the airline could have mitigated. AF will pay hotel/meal costs on top of EU261 if you push. Award tickets qualify for EU261. 3 people = 3 separate claims.
notes: Total recovery ~$3,600 for a family of 3. AF paid after persistent ChatGPT-drafted complaints.

facts: Booked China Eastern flight canceled 2 days prior due to 'company plan' (low load factor). Rebooked manually to same flight next day, resulting in 24-hour delay. Filed complaint for accommodation expenses. Common practice for Chinese airlines to cancel low-load-factor flights.
actual_outcome: Hotel fully reimbursed (based on actual invoices). ~200 RMB fixed compensation for meals/transport. Cash via bank transfer after OP rejected voucher offers.
reusable_lesson: Chinese airlines commonly cancel for commercial reasons. Always reject voucher offers and insist on cash. Keep hotel contact info for post-stay invoice. Communication is entirely by phone.
notes: Insightful DP on Chinese airline practices vs US carriers. Process took 2-3 weeks.

facts: DL flight diverted to ATL due to weather, rebooked to MCO. Bags didn't arrive. BSO wouldn't give claim reference because it was after 6 hours. Spent $127 on replacement items. DL initially rejected reimbursement. Filed DOT and BBB complaints, emailed executives.
actual_outcome: $127 reimbursement + 12,500 SkyMiles. Paid within 2 hours of emailing executive team.
reusable_lesson: Never throw away baggage tags. File BSO claim at airport - don't leave without reference number. Escalate to DOT and/or executive team for quick resolution. Executive emails got response in 2 hours.
notes: Detailed escalation path with specific executive emails. Key: BSO claim must be filed at airport within 6 hours.

facts: Booked 3 nights via Chase Travel (CSR) for $1,800. Multiple issues: bathroom ceiling panel fell off, sofa bed had stink bug corpse, unwashed sheets with dirt, live stink bug on wall. All issues photographed.
actual_outcome: Front desk manager offered 1 night refund ($600). OP accepted. Hilton Twitter team facilitated resolution.
reusable_lesson: Non-elite members can still get partial refunds for severe room issues. Document everything with photos. Twitter/X customer service often faster than phone. CSR booking provides extra support layer.
notes: Multiple serious hygiene/safety issues documented. OP accepted 1-night refund without further push.
